page.title: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Systemic Lupus Erythematosus: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Systemic Lupus Erythematosus: 189 alterations and 276 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Efficacy and safety of total glucosides of paeony in the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus.
Serum homocysteine levels were higher and vitamin B12 levels were lower among individuals with systemic lupus erythematosus.
This study suggests that vaccinations are related to increased risks of systemic lupus erythematosus and rheumatoid arthritis.
Vitamin D supplementation reduced anti-dsDNA positivity on systemic lupus erythematosus and could possibly reduce rheumatoid arthritis recurrence.
Tolerogenic probiotics Lactobacillus delbrueckii and Lactobacillus rhamnosus promote anti-inflammatory profile.
Astragalus injection may be a useful approach for enhancing the efficacy of system lupus erythematoosus treatment.
Combined aerobic and resistance training improves physical and executive functions in women with systemic lupus erythematosus.
Curcumin mitigates systemic lupus erythematosus by suppressing double-negative T cells through cell apoptosis.
Daily consumption of green tea extracts for 12 weeks improves the systemic lupus erythematosus disease activity as well as some aspects of quality of life.
Deficiency of 25(OH)D3, a potentially modifiable risk factor, independently predicted cognitive impairment in SLE patients.
DHEA improves symptoms of systemic lupus erythematosus.
DHEA is well tolerated, clinically beneficial and reduces the need for prednisone in patients with systemic lupus erythematosus.
DHEA is well-tolerated and significantly reduces the number of SLE flares in women with mild-to-moderate systemic lupus erythematosus.
DHEA significantly reduces serum levels of proinflammatory cytokines in women witih systemic lupus erythematosus.
DHEA supplementation improves symptoms in patients with system lupus erythematosus.
Effect of low-level gallium aluminum arsenide laser therapy on the chewing performance and pain perception of patients with systemic lupus erythematosus.
Effects of 12-week whole-body vibration exercise on fatigue, functional ability and quality of life in women with systemic lupus erythematosus.
Efficacy of high-intensity laser therapy on arthropathy of the hands in patients with systemic lupus erythematosus.
EPA and DHA have therapeutic value in the treatment of systemic lupus erythmeatosus.
EPA and DHA induces prolonged remission of systemic lupus erythematosus without side effects.
Exercise training can attenuate the inflammatory milieu in women with systemic lupus erythematosus.
Gentian improves outcome in patients with systemic lupus erythemaatous treated with prednisone.
Hepatitis B vaccination is associated with a wide range of autoimmune diseases.
Hepatitis B vaccination significantly increases the risk of a wide range of autoimmune diseases.
In patients with systemic lupus erythematosus low vitamin D was associated with a higher disease activity and an increase in serum vitamin D was associated with reduced disease activity over time.
Indole-3 carbinol may be beneficial in the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus.
Indole-3-carbinol could modulate the gene expression involved in wound healing in systemic lupus erythematosus cases and healthy controls.
Omega-3 Fatty Acids improve symptoms of systemic lupus erythematosus and may improve cardiovascular function.
Omega-3 fish oil reduces the severity of symptoms in patients with systemic lupus erythematosus.
Pcynogenol significantly reduces severity of symptoms in patients with systemic lupus erythematosus.
Proton pump inhibitors induce changes in the gut microbiome composition of systemic lupus erythematosus patients.
Specific changes in vitamin D and calcium homeostasis in SLE patients may be responsible for the severity of symptoms.
Statin drug use is associated with a Lupus-Like syndrome.
Statin-induced lupus has been identified within a nationwide pharmacovigilance database.
Supplementation with Pycnogenol® may offer advantages and management possibilities for patients with vasculitis diseases allowing to avoid more potentially dangerous drug treatments.
Systemic lupus erythematosus patients given fish oil supplementation demonstrated improvement in their PGA, RAND SF-36, and circulating inflammatory markers.
Systemic lupus erythematosus patients may have lower serontin levels due to enhanced tryptophan degradation associated with their condition.
Systemic lupus erythematosus related to hepatitis B vaccine has been reported.
TaVNS resulted in significantly reduced pain, fatigue and joint scores in systemic lupus erythematosus.
The association between common foods, such as oranges and apples, with specific microorganisms reported to be decreased in SLE could be of great importance for these patients.

Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

clinical_trials.title: Double-blind Placebo Controlled Study to Evaluate the Effect of NAD+ Boosting With Nicotinamide Riboside on Immunometabolism and Immunity in Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
Nicotinamide Treatment for Lupus-associated Skin Lesions in Lupus Erythematosus

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Spontaneous drug-free remission: 2-10% in some cohorts over 5 years; disease-free survival in 15-20% at 20 years; renal lupus (LN) progression to ESRD in 10-20% within 20 years on standard care
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Triple therapy (hydroxychloroquine + MMF + belimumab): 43% complete renal response at 52 weeks (BLISS-LN); voclosporin + MMF: 40.8% complete renal response; anifrolumab (anti-IFNAR): 47.8% BICLA response in active SLE (TULIP-2); aggressive treat-to-target achieves low disease activity in 60-70%
remission.gap_size: Large — hydroxychloroquine is dramatically underprescribed; newer biologics (belimumab, anifrolumab) reach very few eligible patients
remission.barrier_detail: Hydroxychloroquine (the safest, most evidence-based lupus drug) was underprescribed even before COVID-19 controversy; biologics (belimumab, anifrolumab) approved since 2011 and 2021 respectively but used in <15% of eligible patients; racial disparities profound (Black/Hispanic patients have 2-3x higher disease severity and mortality but lower biologic access)
remission.last_soc_change: 2021 — anifrolumab (Saphnelo, anti-IFN-type I) FDA approved — first new mechanism in decade; voclosporin (Lupkynis) for LN approved 2021; belimumab since 2011; hydroxychloroquine as backbone since 1950s; mycophenolate since 2000
remission.outdated_treatments: Cyclophosphamide for mild-moderate LN (MMF proven equally effective with less toxicity in ALMS trial; cyclophosphamide retained for severe/rapidly progressive LN only); azathioprine induction therapy for active proliferative LN (inferior to MMF in RCTs); high-dose OCS as sole therapy for months without steroid-sparing agent
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Vitamin D optimisation (lupus patients characteristically deficient; observational data on disease activity reduction); omega-3 supplementation (anti-inflammatory, some trial data); N-acetylcysteine (NAC) — Phase 2 signal for fatigue and disease activity; exercise (aquatic and aerobic — reduces fatigue without triggering flares)
remission.frontier_signal: CAR-T cell therapy (German case series: 5/5 patients with refractory SLE achieved drug-free remission — 2024 landmark data); CD19-directed CAR-T inducing B cell depletion and subsequent immune reset; obinutuzumab (anti-CD20) for LN Phase 3 (NOBILITY trial); dapirolizumab pegol (anti-CD40L); deucravacitinib (TYK2) for SLE Phase 3 positive
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
